lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Hello. Have you spoken to Councillor Andrews yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Please hurry. You'll find him in Kourend Castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened here?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: She's... she's dead... Thana...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do to help?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: I... Thank you for the offer. You can't help though. She's gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do to help?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: I... I don't know...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. Let's start with what actually happened. Take a moment to collect yourself and then tell me everything.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Okay... Thank you...

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: I'm Mori... I was going to the Library with my friend, Thana. Suddenly, I felt a strange power all through me and then... she... she...

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: ...she just died!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just like that? Was she ill?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: The people of Arceuus don't get ill. We're immortal. We have been for a thousand years, ever since the Ascent of Arceuus. This shouldn't happen... This can't happen...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Ascent of Arceuus?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Yes... A long time ago, our leader, Trobin Arceuus, used the Dark Altar to discover the secret of immortality. He came to us and let us share in this secret.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: The events of that day became known as the Ascent of Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: But something's gone wrong... Thana is dead and I don't know why!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We should let someone know about this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We should let someone know about this.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: You... You're right. We should let the Kourend Council know. Could you help? I don't want to leave her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course I'll help.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Thank you... Could you head to Kourend Castle and let Councillor Andrews know what's happened? He's in charge of the council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do. I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I have something else to do.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Oh... Okay...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shouldn't get involved.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Oh... Okay...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Good day to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's been a death in Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: I'm sorry, but as I already told you, we can't help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Good day to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's been a death in Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: A death? Well that doesn't seem right. The people of Arceuus don't just die. They can't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've seen it with my own eyes. A citizen of Arceuus has fallen. What should we do?

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Well I don't really know what we can do. The Arceuus family have chosen to keep their knowledge secret from the rest of us. We have no expertise in this area.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'll do nothing?

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: If I could do something, I would. However, I'm afraid this is out of our hands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. I suppose I'd better tell Mori.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Hello. Have you spoken to Lord Arceuus yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Please don't take too long. You'll find Lord Arceuus in the Tower of Magic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Hello. Have you spoken to Councillor Andrews yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have. The council won't be helping I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Oh... I shouldn't be surprised I guess... The council only care about helping themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like you don't like the council much.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: The council are supposed to rule Kourend, but they never do anything for the people. They shouldn't even exist anyway. They were only meant to rule until the last king's daughter came of age.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happened?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: The council declared her unfit for rule not long after the king died. She disappeared soon after and no one knows what became of her. Now the council are the permanent rulers of Kourend.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: It was a waste of time even going to them. They were never going to care about Thana.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should we do now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should we do now?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Well... if the Council won't help us, maybe Lord Arceuus will. He doesn't show his face in public much these days but he can't ignore the death of one of his own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good idea. Where can I find him?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: He'll be in the Tower of Magic, north west of here.

lines.speaker: Tower Mage
lines.text: Somebody deal with these souls!

lines.speaker: Tower Mage
lines.text: Clear off!

lines.speaker: Tower Mage
lines.text: Get out of it!

lines.speaker: Tower Mage
lines.text: Somebody deal with this!

lines.speaker: Tower Mage
lines.text: Someone sort out these souls!

lines.speaker: Tower Mage
lines.text: We need to deal with these souls!

lines.speaker: Tower Mage
lines.text: Thank you for helping with those souls. You should head upstairs and speak with Asteros Arceuus. He might be able to fill you in on what's happening here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to speak to Lord Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Tower Mage
lines.text: We're currently facing an issue in the Tower of Magic. No one may enter until the issue is resolved.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But it's important, there's been a death in Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Tower Mage
lines.text: A death? This isn't good. The death you speak of may be connected to the issue we are currently facing. You'd better go in.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: Please hurry to Mount Karuulm. The Tasakaal may be the only ones who can save us. You'll find the volcano on the other side of Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: Thank you for helping us out with those souls. As you can see, we're facing quite the crisis.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand. What's going on?

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: I'm afraid we don't quite know ourselves yet. A short while ago, our instruments detected fluctuations in power from the Dark Altar.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: Before we could do anything, we all felt a strange loss of power, as if the Altar had failed altogether.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: My father was quick to act, using himself as a conduit to channel what little power the Altar had left.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: It seems the loss in power had an interesting impact on our experiments though. Some trapped souls that we were working to free were suddenly released.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: Alas, whatever happened seemed to cause them great pain, making them to go feral. They are now at peace though, thanks to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm afraid I have some bad news which is likely related to this. A citizen of Arceuus has died.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: A death? No! My father acted as fast as he could, but it sounds like he was not fast enough. This is a dark day for Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: Tell me, who has fallen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thana. Mori is with her next to the pond.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: Dear Thana, you did not deserve this fate. Thank you for bringing this terrible news to us. I will make sure someone is sent out to take care of Thana and Mori.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still don't understand how this happened.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: Ever since the Ascent of Arceuus, our people have been kept alive by the Dark Altar. Its power sustains our bodies in the same way yours is sustained by food and drink.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: Without the power of the Dark Altar, there's nothing to keep our people alive. The loss in power from the Altar will be what killed Thana.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: The Altar is currently giving out a fraction of the power it normally would. The only thing keeping us alive is my father. His strength won't last forever though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do we do?

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: If we can't find and fix whatever has caused this loss of power, our people are surely doomed. Now that those souls have been dealt with, we can perform tests both here and at the Altar to try and find the problem.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: Time is not on our side though, and I fear we won't be fast enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help?

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: Well... There is a secret known only to a few of us. At any other time, I would never dream of telling an outsider.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: But our people's lives are in danger and you may well be the only one who can save us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this secret?

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: It's believed by most that the knowledge of immortality was discovered by my father. The truth is, the discovery was not his.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: To the west of here, lies the sulphur volcano of Mount Karuulm. Within live the Tasakaal, three ancient beings with power over life and death. They granted my father the knowledge that led to the Ascent of Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you think they can help us?

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: Their knowledge of the Dark Altar far exceeds ours. If anyone can help us, it's them. Ideally I'd send one of our own, but I have no one to spare. You are the only one who can help us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better not waste any time then.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: Thank you friend. You'll find Mount Karuulm on the other side of Lovakengj. Please hurry.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: The human returns.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want me to do again?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Free the trapped soul.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: You'll find it in a grave.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: By the battlefront to the south.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Human.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Leave us.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Your presence is not desired.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I need your help! I was sent by Asteros Arceuus, son of Lord Trobin Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: We know that name.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: The name of a liar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Liar?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: They came to us. Two of them.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: The brother and the husband.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Her time was running out. They wanted to give her more.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: We granted them knowledge. To give her more time.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: But they misused it. And the world suffered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This knowledge? You mean immortality?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: In a way. But it was not meant for that.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Time was what it was meant for. But time still runs out.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: They put an end to time. They endangered the balance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What balance?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: For every death, there must be life.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: For every life, there must be death.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: This balance is what we serve.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: For without balance, the world burns.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're very confusing, you know that right?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Perhaps.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Perhaps our knowledge is beyond your understanding.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well thankfully I didn't come here for knowledge. I came here for help. The Dark Altar is failing and the people of Arceuus are in danger. I need you to help me find the cause.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: The people of Arceuus have had their time.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: This will restore balance.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Why should we help them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *sigh* It can never be easy can it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. If you help me save the people of Arceuus, then I will help you.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: You assume we need help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've done this enough times to know that everyone wants something, even the masters of life and death.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Interesting. Maybe the human is not as stupid as it looks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I've been known to have my moments.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Very well human.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: We admit, the Arceuus people do have their uses.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Their work with souls has been useful in helping to maintain the balance.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: We will help them. But first, you will serve us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excellent. What do you need from me?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: To the south lies a battlefront.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: There is a grave there. Within it lies a soul, trapped inside a corpse.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Free this soul. In return, we will provide our help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you just want me to kill a zombie?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: A zombie is soulless. An empty shell, reanimated by magic.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: This is a soul. Trapped in this world, when it should have long departed.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Its existence is an affront to the balance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems pretty simple.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Which is why it falls to you.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Consider it a test.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: If you pass.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: We may have further use for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. Very well. I'll go and free this soul.

sections.title: Returning to the Tasakaal after freeing the trapped soul

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: The human returns.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Go to the Dark Altar.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Find and destroy the construct.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: And Arceuus will be saved.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: The human returns.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've dealt with the trapped soul.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: You have done well.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: The balance has been restored.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: And now we shall help you.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: While you were busy, so were we.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: We have found the cause of your problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: You said the Altar was failing.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: This is not correct.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: The Altar is fine, but its power has been redirected.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Redirected where?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: To a construct, built by a mortal.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Right next to the Altar.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Find it. Destroy it.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: And Arceuus will be saved.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I just have to go to the Dark Altar, find this device and then destroy it. Sounds easy enough.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Then you have what you need.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: You can leave us.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Go and save your people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There. That's the device destroyed. The people of Arceuus should be safe now. I'd better go to the Tower of Magic and check.

lines.speaker: Asteros Arceuus
lines.text: Whatever you did, it seems to have worked. Everything is back to normal. You should talk to my father, he'll want to thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lord Arceuus?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: You're the one that saved our people. On behalf of all of Arceuus, I thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So everything is okay now?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: It seems so. A short while ago I felt the power of the Dark Altar return to normal levels. I was safely able to stop my ritual and let the Altar work on its own again.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Now tell me, what was the cause of this and how did you stop it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your son recommended that I speak to the Tasakaal. They had me perform a task for them but after that they agreed to help. They didn't seem to like you much though.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Well that's no surprise. The Tasakaal seem to feel that only they deserve immortality. It's rather hypocritical of them. It clearly didn't stop them helping you though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They were reluctant, but they admitted that the people of Arceuus were useful to them.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Such is their way. They act superior to us, but the reality is they'll work with anyone if they think it will further their aims.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: So how did they help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They found out that the power of the Altar was being redirected into a device hidden nearby. I found the device and destroyed it.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: A device? What was it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure, but the Tasakaal said it was made by a mortal.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: The Tasakaal view everyone who isn't one of them as a mere mortal, even those of us who have also achieved immortality. That doesn't narrow down the options very much.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: This is most concerning though. If someone deliberately sabotaged the Altar, who knows what else they might do. I'll have someone recover the remains of this device. Maybe we can work out where it came from.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: For now though, I'd like to once again thank you for your help. Today was a dark day for our people but without you it would have been our end. Please accept these gifts. They are the least you deserve.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Now, can I help you further?
